The opening Of the childrens
playground at the corner of River
side and Monroe has been post
poned until July 2S.


The awning of the Riverside ho-
tel burned about s o'clock yester-


day morning and attracted a large
crowd. The cause of the fire is
unknown.


Burrel ]. Cramer, Of South Bend,


Ind, grant past president of the
grand nest of Owls, is in the city
and Wednesday night will visit
with the local nest of Owls.
Special services to the Woodmen
of the World and Women of Wood
craft were held at the First Univer-
salist church yesterday morning.
Both lodges attended in a body.
Rev. Downey devoted large part
of his sermon to the spirit of fra
ternity


Spokane Christian Endeavor So
cieties united last night at the First
Presbyterian church in all echo"


meeting of the international con-
vention held in Seattle. The
church was crowded with young


people. Rev. w. A. Hunter presided


and introduced the returned dele
gates, who spoke briefly on the
benefits and features of interest of
the great gathering. Rev. r. A..
Smith, Rev. E. H. Mills and Rev.


M. E. Dunn delivered addresses.
1t is planned this year to oper-
ate 80 large granaries in connec-


ton with the Spokane & Inland
electric system. By this means it


is believed the Zig grain crop can


be better taken care of and stored


until sold. These grain houses will


be operated by the Washington


Grain & Milling Co. Of this city;
Galbraith & Bacon, of Palouse; E.


]. Doneen, of Oakesdal. The In


land will have new locomotives


delivered by Sept. l and with these


additions it Is expected all the
grain can be handled.
